126554.explanation: Referendums are a good way to present municipal council decisions to society if enough residents request it.

126557.explanation: Entrepreneurs know best what is needed for their business, which is why GroenLinks-PvdA believes they should decide this themselves. It is important that this does not come at the expense of livability in neighborhoods outside the center.

126559.explanation: Too many enormous distribution centers have been built in the Netherlands in recent years. They spoil the landscape, and much of the work is done by poorly paid migrant laborers. As far as we are concerned, we should stop the arrival of more distribution centers.

126562.explanation: The fine dust in smoke from fireplaces and wood stoves creates an unhealthy environment. People with conditions like asthma suffer greatly, and for more and more people, the unhealthy environment is a problem. We want less burning and eventually a complete stop.

126563.explanation: We want everyone to use energy sensibly. Heating outdoor air is a waste of energy. You often see blankets over chairs for people who want to sit outside.

126564.explanation: It is important and fun to celebrate the New Year together. It is good if the municipality organizes something for this. In Wageningen, this will happen with a drone show.

126565.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA considers it important that waste is properly processed so that raw materials can be reused. Free bulky waste collection can contribute to this. The current system, where this is free a few times per year, is good. People who discard a lot of waste should pay more.

126566.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants a lively city. Celebrations and festivals are part of that. Too much noise causes hearing damage and nuisance for people and animals. Therefore, we believe the municipality should set stricter noise standards. Festivals with slightly lower volume are also fun, maybe even more fun.

126567.explanation: People with disabilities must be fully able to participate in society, just like others. That is why GroenLinks-PvdA believes entrepreneurs should receive help from the municipality to make their shop, restaurant, or café more accessible for people with disabilities.

126568.explanation: A local broadcaster is important. The current funding they receive is sufficient.

126569.explanation: A pleasant and safe school is important for children and teachers. That is why the municipality continues to invest. We prefer renovation over demolition. This way, materials are reused well. School buildings can also be used by others. Together, we ensure healthy and sustainable schools.

126571.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants a Wageningen where everyone can participate in sports. If a club cannot make certain investments or keep sports affordable for people with less money, the municipality should help with that.

126573.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to improve safety. That is why the municipal enforcement team has been expanded in recent years. Now it is not necessary to enlarge the team further.

126574.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants everyone to feel safe on the streets. Camera surveillance seems safe but usually is not. We prefer to prevent dangerous situations. Together with residents, we look at unsafe places and address them.

126576.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA currently does not believe that enforcement officers (BOAs) in Wageningen need batons, especially when the police can quickly respond if something happens. If BOAs themselves request batons, this can be reconsidered later.

126577.explanation: Lending money to first-time homebuyers makes houses more expensive and disrupts the market. As a result, first-time buyers also incur more debt. It is far more important to ensure there are enough affordable homes in Wageningen. Therefore, GroenLinks-PvdA is fully committed to this.

126578.explanation: There are too few social rental homes in Wageningen. GroenLinks-PvdA is ensuring that more are built. People from Wageningen or with ties to the city are given priority. This also improves the flow on the housing market.

126582.explanation: The shortage of student housing in Wageningen is smaller than in other student cities. The rooms are also reasonably affordable. GroenLinks-PvdA provides for new permanent student housing if needed, such as at Costerweg, Marijkeweg, and Mansholtlaan.

126583.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA trusts residents. We help people instead of working against them. Everyone gets the chance to participate and develop talents. We do this with as few rules as possible.

126585.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes that no one should live in poverty. Therefore, we have started new policies consisting of one budget so residents in poverty can also participate. We look at income and fixed costs. This way, everyone gets the help they need.

126588.explanation: Data shows that a lower maximum speed limit makes traffic in our municipality much safer. Especially for cycling children and elderly people with limited mobility. It is also quieter on the streets and the air becomes cleaner.

126589.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants more neighborhoods with parking permits. This ensures that neighborhood residents can always find a spot. There is also more space for greenery, play areas, and safe streets. People with mobility issues can park nearby.

126590.explanation: Wageningen wants to do a lot for the climate. Traffic still emits too much CO2. An environmental zone helps reduce CO2. It also results in cleaner and healthier air.

126591.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA believes it is important that everyone receives the care they need. A healthcare provider who knows the patient’s culture or religion can deliver better care. It is important that someone feels safe, can speak in their own language, and is well understood.

126593.explanation: Period poverty is a big problem, also in Wageningen. GroenLinks-PvdA wants the municipality to make menstrual products available for free at public places such as schools, libraries, pharmacies, and asylum seeker centers.

126594.explanation: Because Wageningen has had its finances well managed for years, a higher property tax (OZB) is not necessary now.

126595.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA wants to keep the dog tax. This allows investment in facilities for dogs, such as off-leash areas. Because of this, Wageningen has become the most dog-friendly municipality in the Netherlands. We want to keep it that way.

126596.explanation: We want tourists to feel welcome in Wageningen. A higher tourist tax would work against that.

126597.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit (status holders) often have no home of their own and no family to fall back on. They can become homeless as a result. They are in the same vulnerable position as other people who get priority for social housing.

126599.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

126601.explanation: Wageningen currently has 3 coffeeshops which more than meet demand. Because the production of cannabis is still prohibited, no extra coffeeshops are needed.

126603.explanation: We want to build houses close together in the city. This way, a lot of green space remains outside the city. If we build higher in some places, and do that attractively, we can also maintain more green spaces in the city.

126604.explanation: There is a large shortage of homes and many people in Wageningen live in homes larger than necessary. Therefore, we want to make it easier to rent out rooms if they are well maintained and the rent is fair.

126606.explanation: With the ‘Vision Bouwde Kom’, the municipal council has decided where building is allowed. A few areas lie outside the built-up area, near Haarweg and the Nieuwe Kanaal. Outside these boundaries, the green space remains. This creates room for expansion without harming the countryside.

126608.explanation: GroenLinks-PvdA finds it important that the center remains easily accessible. Parking and bicycle storage need better arrangements and the route from the bus station should be clearer. We agree on what is needed together with entrepreneurs and cultural institutions.

126554.explanation: Residents have various ways to get topics on the municipal council’s agenda or to influence decision-making. Once a decision is made by the municipal council, we believe that decision should not be reopened for debate.

126557.explanation: The Stadspartij believes there are too many regulations, especially in the city center. For example, we think that municipal rules regarding opening hours should be relaxed. We also focus on extra promotion of our shopping evening and shopping Sunday in surrounding municipalities.

126559.explanation: The Stadspartij believes distribution centers do not fit into the Wageningen landscape.

126562.explanation: The Stadspartij opposes a complete ban on the use of fireplaces and wood stoves. However, we do believe that the regulations on the use of wood stoves should be better enforced.

126563.explanation: The Stadspartij opposes banning terrace heating. It is the responsibility of each entrepreneur to run their business as energy-efficiently as possible. The municipality can call on them to use heating as little as possible.

126564.explanation: The Stadspartij sees offering a light and laser show and organizing local activities as an important part of a future New Year's Eve. The municipality must therefore actively work to make this available to everyone in Wageningen.

126565.explanation: In Wageningen, it is possible to bring bulky waste to the municipal waste station using an address-bound pass. The Stadspartij supports continuing the current procedure.

126566.explanation: The Stadspartij wants to ensure that the maximum noise standard at events is lowered.

126567.explanation: The Stadspartij finds it important that companies take measures to make their buildings more accessible to people with disabilities where applicable. However, we believe a company is itself responsible for its employees and any visitors.

126568.explanation: The Stadspartij sees the local broadcaster as a valuable addition to Wageningen and its surroundings. Therefore, we believe the local broadcaster can be used more by the municipality, even if this requires extra funding.

126569.explanation: The Stadspartij believes more money should be reserved for maintenance of school buildings. These funds should also be used for sustainability improvements.

126571.explanation: Sports must be accessible and affordable for everyone and remain so. Therefore, we stand for organizational and financial investment in sports.

126573.explanation: The Stadspartij wants financial space as soon as possible for more BOAs on the street. This will also improve the recovery of damages caused by vandalism through broader deployment of BOAs.

126574.explanation: Work must be done on good lighting and visibility of safety measures. For example, we are not opposed in advance to camera surveillance at large-scale events, in the center, and in areas with much nuisance.

126576.explanation: The Stadspartij wants financial space as soon as possible for more BOAs on the street equipped better to act. This will also improve the recovery of damages caused by vandalism through broader deployment of BOAs.

126577.explanation: The Stadspartij wants to realize affordable rental and owner-occupied homes as soon as possible. We see large-scale lending for buying a house primarily as the task of banks, not the municipality.

126578.explanation: The Stadspartij wants to give residents of Wageningen priority when finding a rental or owner-occupied home. We make no distinction between first-time buyers and people moving within the market.

126582.explanation: Given the current decline in university enrollments, we believe enough new student housing has been built in recent years relative to the demand for student rooms. We will always promote the sustainability of existing student housing.

126583.explanation: The Stadspartij believes everyone can contribute to our municipality according to their abilities. A (small) reciprocal contribution may be asked for in return for social assistance benefits, for example in the form of volunteer work.

126585.explanation: The Stadspartij believes everyone should be able to participate. Therefore, we will continue to critically examine the income limit and will advocate where necessary to raise it.

126588.explanation: The Stadspartij believes that the speed of car traffic in residential neighborhoods and other built-up areas should be limited to a maximum of 30 kilometers per hour. On through routes, to maintain traffic flow, the maximum speed remains 50 kilometers per hour.

126589.explanation: Introducing more parking permits only contributes to increasing regulatory pressure in Wageningen. Therefore, we only support parking permits in places where there are many visitors from outside.

126590.explanation: We believe that implementing an environmental zone does not suit the size of a city like Wageningen.

126591.explanation: For professional care, the municipality must ensure that it is provided in a clear, transparent, and fair manner. Care and services must be of (demonstrably) good quality. Everyone who needs care should be able to rely on receiving the right support.

126593.explanation: The Stadspartij believes we should not only act reactively but actively focus on prevention and raising awareness of what inclusion means for the residents of Wageningen.

126594.explanation: The Stadspartij has been working for years to prevent further increases in property tax (OZB). The OZB has risen (too) sharply in recent years. We believe that a municipal financial shortfall should not be compensated by raising the OZB.

126595.explanation: The Stadspartij views the dog tax as an unfair pet tax. Moreover, most of the money goes to the general funds and is not spent on dog facilities.

126596.explanation: Wageningen is and remains the city of liberation. As a result, we receive many tourists each year. However, we do not see an increase in tourist tax as a solution to a budget shortfall.

126597.explanation: We give priority to residents of Wageningen when looking for a purchase or rental home. We make no distinction between first-time buyers and those moving homes.

126599.explanation: The Stadspartij wants solar panels primarily on building roofs and not through solar parks on the ground where this comes at the expense of the already limited land area in the municipality of Wageningen and the vulnerable surrounding countryside.

126601.explanation: The Stadspartij believes there are currently sufficient coffeeshops in Wageningen.

126603.explanation: The Stadspartij believes that to remain a livable and vibrant city, we must build inward and upward with housing construction.

126604.explanation: The Stadspartij is committed to realizing affordable homes for starters as quickly as possible. Therefore, we support housing subdivision.

126606.explanation: The Stadspartij believes that to remain a livable and vibrant city, we must build inward and upward with housing construction. This option preserves current green spaces and may even make it possible to create more greenery.

126608.explanation: For better accessibility to the city center and safer traffic movements in the surrounding neighborhoods, the Stadspartij wants to maintain and increase the number of parking spaces around the city center where necessary. We believe that the planned parking garage by the Prison Tower should be built as soon as possible.

126554.explanation: A strong municipality is transparent, accessible, and decisive. D66 therefore strengthens and renews local democracy. We find it important that residents have influence over their own environment. For this, we use citizen councils and lower the thresholds for local referenda.

126557.explanation: Hospitality, retail, and creative businesses get space from D66 to grow. This also includes extended opening hours. Moreover, this helps prevent possible nuisance by avoiding peak loads.

126559.explanation: Innovation is in our DNA. From startups on the campus to established companies in the region: Wageningen is a testing ground. D66 connects scientists, professionals, and residents. We make the city a Living Lab: a place where ideas are tested and collaboration is natural.

126562.explanation: D66 wants to tackle air pollution by reducing motorized traffic and raising awareness about wood burning and barbecues. This is how we build a city that is healthy inside and out.

126563.explanation: D66 gives the hospitality sector room to operate. However, we make agreements that this is done sustainably, such as using blankets and heat cushions. This is more effective than banning.

126564.explanation: D66 wants to give a more modern interpretation to the year-end closure. So that it causes no nuisance to pets and residents, but we can all enjoy and celebrate together. A laser show could be a nice way to achieve this.

126565.explanation: The basic principle is that the polluter pays. In Wageningen, there are options to have bulky waste collected at home to prevent it from being dumped in nature.

126566.explanation: The current permit rules and maximum noise standards are a compromise between enabling great events in Wageningen and limiting nuisance for nearby residents. Attention must also be given to hearing protection for young people and effects on animals.

126567.explanation: Businesses have their own responsibility to make their buildings accessible. D66 wants to invest in improving the accessibility of sidewalks, bike paths, and government buildings. D66 will also continually raise awareness about accessibility among entrepreneurs and organizations.

126568.explanation: D66 finds it important for local society and democracy that local media continue to exist. To make this possible, additional support from the municipality is necessary.

126569.explanation: The municipality has the task of building and maintaining sufficient school buildings. D66 wants school buildings to be a healthy and sustainable environment for children and teachers.

126571.explanation: D66 believes it is important that everyone can participate in sports. The municipality invests in sports fields and facilitates associations. D66 does not consider it the municipality’s task to financially support sports clubs beyond that.

126573.explanation: Safety is freedom. D66 ensures a city where you feel safe—on the street, online, and at home. We invest in social safety, good lighting, and safe crossings. Places perceived as unsafe by residents, such as the bus station, will be made safer.

126574.explanation: An important aspect of safety is feeling free without the sense that someone is watching over your shoulder. Therefore, protecting the privacy of Wageningen residents is essential. This applies to discouraging video surveillance and the use of private drones.

126576.explanation: The safety of enforcement officers (BOAs) is not increased by giving them batons. The chance of escalation and more severe violence against BOAs will only rise. Better protective equipment is then a preferable option.

126577.explanation: D66 wants to enable investments in home sustainability through loans, but general starter loans are very costly and only push housing prices even higher.

126578.explanation: In every project, we build as much social and mid-rent housing as possible. Starters get opportunities, seniors can move on, and students find places in the city or on campus. Housing is more than a roof over your head. It is the foundation for participation, meeting others, and growth.

126582.explanation: As long as pressure on the housing market remains high, D66 considers it necessary to create temporary housing for students.

126583.explanation: Everyone deserves a chance to participate, including those with distance from the labor market. We provide guidance, education, and support—without stigma, with perspective, and retention of personal control. If working is not possible, we ensure appropriate, dignified daytime activities.

126585.explanation: With the Social Participation Budget, we give residents the freedom to choose—for sports, culture, clothing, or socializing. This budget is no longer determined by an income limit, but considers both income and expenses (e.g., high rent or energy bills).

126588.explanation: The street does not belong to the car but to everyone. D66 wants neighborhoods where an 8-year-old can safely bike to school and an 88-year-old can walk independently to the store. Therefore, within built-up areas, we introduce a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h.

126589.explanation: D66 wants a car-free city center and car-reduced neighborhoods. We enable this by creating parking hubs at the city edges. A permit will be required for the necessary parking spaces remaining in the neighborhoods.

126590.explanation: D66 wants a car-free city center as soon as possible. An environmental zone is a good interim step.

126591.explanation: Freedom also means being able to be yourself. D66 stands firm for human rights, equal treatment, and a society without discrimination. We protect what is vulnerable—and strengthen what connects. Freedom requires care. D66 watches over safety and your right to live freely.

126593.explanation: D66 finds it important to pay attention to period poverty. Tampons and sanitary pads are distributed at multiple locations.

126594.explanation: Municipal taxes must be fair and contribute to a strong city. We ensure transparency and accountability because every euro must work for Wageningen. Finances are not spreadsheets but choices. D66 chooses to invest in what matters.

126595.explanation: The dog tax is a targeted tax whose revenues go to the public costs of dog ownership, such as a dog park. D66 agrees that the required funds should be raised by dog owners and not by all taxpayers.

126596.explanation: Wageningen currently has a relatively low tourist tax. The proceeds from a slightly higher tax can be effectively used for better promotion of Wageningen.

126597.explanation: D66 wants to prevent status holders from wandering around unable to start participating in society. To reduce pressure on social housing, we will build 3,000 to 4,000 homes before 2035 with as much social and mid-level rent as possible.

126599.explanation: The future is electric, sunny, and smart. D66 keeps up the pace. Therefore, it is also necessary to equip fields (temporarily) with solar panels. However, we consider the impact on the energy grid in every new project. We also prefer dual use of the land.

126601.explanation: D66 agrees that the sale and possession of small amounts of soft drugs in the Netherlands are not prohibited. This way, consumers do not have to deal with criminal dealers and there is some quality control.

126603.explanation: We mix high-rise and low-rise buildings, infill and expansion. Every neighborhood has space for care, sports, education, greenery, and social interaction. Wageningen suffers from a major housing shortage. Therefore, D66 chooses acceleration. We build faster but do not lose sight of affordability and sustainability.

126604.explanation: Wageningen is a student city, and besides students, many families and singles are also looking for housing. Renting out rooms within a house also contributes to reducing tightness in the housing market.

126606.explanation: Wageningen lies between beautiful green areas. We want to realize the city's growth through both infill and expansion. This must be balanced to prevent the city and outskirts from being completely built over at the expense of green spaces.

126608.explanation: Following the 2025 citizens' council, D66 chooses to set up hubs outside the city center. Here you can park, transfer goods, and take a bike or small-scale public transport to the center. This way, the city center can become car-free.

126554.explanation: We find it important that residents can participate in major decisions. A referendum is one of the options. We would like to see further participation, such as citizen councils, for example for a youth strategy and further city development.

126557.explanation: Wageningen deserves a vibrant nightlife that offers space for young and old, artists, and entrepreneurs. We want the municipality to actively think along about how to prevent nuisance so that entrepreneurs get the space to bring more life to the night.

126559.explanation: Distribution centers bring a lot of traffic. The infrastructure of Wageningen is not suitable for this.

126562.explanation: We commit to banning wood burning in the long term. This prevents pollution and health damage. We offer assistance to residents who depend on wood burning for home heating.

126563.explanation: To keep terraces attractive, we think along and are open to innovation. Traditional terrace heating is not sustainable and can be dangerous, so we want to ban it in the long term.

126564.explanation: A fireworks show creates connection and a festive start to the New Year. We let residents per neighborhood participate in shaping the 'new-style' New Year's celebration.

126565.explanation: It is already possible to have waste collected free of charge three times.

126566.explanation: By setting standards, we can protect everyone from lifelong hearing damage.

126567.explanation: The municipality can advise entrepreneurs on the accessibility of their buildings. Modifications to the building are at the entrepreneur's expense.

126568.explanation: With a strong local broadcaster, we bring the municipality closer to the residents.

126569.explanation: Wageningen is already making good progress in improving school buildings. We will continue this. Investing now pays off in the future.

126571.explanation: Wageningen has a rich sports offering, provided by many different associations. Financial gains can be achieved by encouraging associations to cooperate. As a last resort, the municipality can step in to prevent the sports offering from diminishing.

126573.explanation: Street harassment also occurs in Wageningen. Enforcement officers make themselves visible and ensure they are available as points of contact for unsafe situations.

126574.explanation: Safety is not guaranteed by cameras. They are often expensive and pose privacy risks. We want a structural solution.

126576.explanation: We do not believe this will increase the feeling of safety. Equipping community enforcement officers (BOAs) with batons makes them more part of the aggression.

126577.explanation: Finding a starter home in Wageningen is difficult. Therefore, we must help starters. We want to do this in cooperation with Foodvalley. This way, more funds are unlocked and differences between municipalities are prevented.

126578.explanation: We want to give priority on a limited number of homes to home seekers who are socially or economically tied to Wageningen, thus reducing misallocation. However, this must not come at the expense of urgent shelter and flow-through from the asylum seekers' center (AZC).

126582.explanation: Everyone deserves a place to live. Temporary housing is part of the solution, provided the quality is good. We would not only want to build these homes for students but for everyone in need of independent living space.

126583.explanation: We want 'stepping stone jobs' to offer opportunities to people who find it hard to get work. We see these stepping stone jobs as a way to implement the Participation Act. We encourage people with benefits to use them and thus contribute socially.

126585.explanation: With the social participation budget, which combats poverty, we are already leading in the Netherlands. This scheme is already broadly set up. We want to first see how it works out before considering improvements.

126588.explanation: On all roads except the major access roads, we enforce a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h. We want to investigate whether reducing speeds on access roads to 30 km/h can reduce cut-through traffic through Wageningen.

126589.explanation: Private use of public space does not have to be free. The larger the car, the more expensive. Parking permits are also a way to stimulate sustainable mobility. The money is invested in public amenities in neighborhoods, public transport, greenery, and sustainability.

126590.explanation: Many people live and walk in the city center. By banning fine dust and exhaust emissions in the center, we protect residents' health. Alternative supply methods are facilitated by the municipality.

126591.explanation: Everyone has the freedom to make their own choices. Wageningen purchases care together with other municipalities, looking at quality, availability, and price. Care outside this scope is not paid for by the municipality.

126593.explanation: Wageningen is a social and inclusive city. For those who need it, free tampons and sanitary pads are available at schools, associations, community centers, asylum seeker centers, and care institutions. This breaks the taboo and helps those in need.

126594.explanation: Wageningen is a city full of ambition. By investing now, Wageningen remains an attractive city and saves costs in the future.

126595.explanation: Wageningen invests in cleaning up dog waste areas, setting up playgrounds, and providing poop bags and trash bins. The dog tax is a good means to finance this.

126596.explanation: Tourists contribute to a lively city full of hospitality, culture, music, and art. Tourism pressure on Wageningen is low, so we do not want to discourage it further. The revenues are invested in the city.

126597.explanation: Wageningen is a social city that contributes to the flow of refugees from asylum seekers' centers. We give refugees a good start by providing guidance and promoting integration with the city. By making 40% of new housing social, we ensure housing for everyone.

126599.explanation: We believe that all usable roofs, carports, and parking lots in Wageningen should be used for solar panels. In rural areas, we prefer wind turbines over solar fields due to the space consumption of solar fields.

126601.explanation: Like liquor stores, we want to legalize coffeeshops. This reduces their 'secretive' nature and creates more social safety. Coffeeshops are not located near schools, care institutions, or busy locations.

126603.explanation: In the city center and neighborhoods, we see the possibility to build between 5-7 floors, and in areas like the 'oksel' we see opportunities to build as high as the tower flats. We pay attention to sightlines, sunlight, and preventing excessive paving.

126604.explanation: Everyone studying in Wageningen should have the space to live here. We want to promote this with more affordable rooms and studios, adding floors at suitable locations, and clear rental permits to prevent nuisance.

126606.explanation: In some neighborhoods we can expand, such as between Haarweg and Gamma, and between Wageningen and Bennekom. In other neighborhoods like Sahara, Wageningen Hoog, and the harbor area, we can densify. We will not sacrifice nature reserves for housing.

126608.explanation: In the center, we need to move away from an environment designed for cars. A parking garage just outside the inner city with walking routes to the center should support this. Spaces for disabled people will remain available.

126554.explanation: Residents should always be able to share their opinions. We do not need expensive organized referendums for this. The municipality can hold similar participation moments and polls among its residents. The VVD supports this to also increase involvement with our city.

126557.explanation: The VVD wants hospitality businesses to decide their own opening hours, with respect for the surroundings.

126559.explanation: The VVD believes we can use more employment opportunities for youth and practically educated people in Wageningen well. The VVD supports economic growth to which new entrepreneurs contribute.

126562.explanation: The VVD expects our residents to burn responsibly to prevent nuisance.

126563.explanation: The VVD is against a ban; entrepreneurs choose for themselves.

126564.explanation: The VVD believes residents and entrepreneurs can organize parties themselves. If a fireworks display is part of it, the municipality can possibly facilitate this.

126565.explanation: Bulky waste often occurs during renovations or moving. Taking out your trash can be budgeted and scheduled accordingly.

126566.explanation: Stricter noise standards will not work according to the VVD if they are not enforced. Parties should be possible, but noise standards are known per permit in advance and must therefore be enforced, otherwise neighbors experience more nuisance than agreed.

126567.explanation: According to the VVD, this can be done in consultation with the entrepreneurs.

126568.explanation: Local media must also remain independent from the municipality.

126569.explanation: Not all school buildings need improvement in the next 4 years.

126571.explanation: The municipality must actively support sports clubs. Not only financially but also with other requests for help. Sports have needed more attention in Wageningen in recent years than it has received from the municipality.

126573.explanation: The VVD prefers more police officers rather than enforcement officers (BOAs) to focus on safety.

126574.explanation: There is currently too much reliance on the camera surveillance that entrepreneurs have installed themselves. Camera surveillance, for example at entertainment venues, has a preventive effect that increases safety.

126576.explanation: The VVD prefers more police for this rather than BOAs, because only the police have the authority to use force under strict conditions.

126577.explanation: The VVD wants to help starters get a home.

126578.explanation: The VVD wants Wageningen residents to have priority in times of scarcity.

126582.explanation: The VVD supports fast construction of homes, especially for starters, not just for students.

126583.explanation: The VVD believes those who can, should give something back to society.

126585.explanation: The VVD wants aid targeted at those who really need it.

126588.explanation: The VVD chooses safety with tailored measures; not 30 km/h everywhere. We want to address unsafe traffic spots and enforce behavior.

126589.explanation: The VVD prefers to invest in a parking garage to solve the parking problem.

126590.explanation: The VVD is against introducing zero-emission zones. Shops would have difficulty getting their goods inside and visitors could no longer come by car. The VVD prefers innovation and smooth traffic flow, in good coordination with our city center entrepreneurs.

126591.explanation: The VVD wants free choice of care provider for everyone. The offer must be available to all. The VVD wants care to be close and personal.

126593.explanation: The VVD wants targeted support through poverty schemes. Tampons and sanitary pads could be made available at various locations. This way we invest in everyone being able to participate!

126594.explanation: The VVD keeps the property tax (ozb) low; no increase.

126595.explanation: The VVD wants to abolish the dog tax. We have actively proposed this several times in recent years.

126596.explanation: The VVD preferably keeps the tourist tax low. However, when it can be used for qualitative improvement, that is a possibility.

126597.explanation: There are multiple groups with urgency in being assigned social housing. Think of situations where someone is about to become homeless, with or without children. The VVD believes status holders should not automatically be at the top of this list.

126601.explanation: The VVD agrees with the current regulations on regulation and enforcement.

126603.explanation: The VVD sees possibilities for this only just outside the built-up area in Wageningen. It is a quick way to create many homes. The VVD critically assesses this based on sightlines from existing buildings and the overall neighborhood image.

126604.explanation: The VVD observes that room-by-room renting, where single-family homes are used as student complexes, causes a lot of nuisance in practice. A total ban goes too far for the VVD but we want to tighten these rules to address nuisance for neighbors.

126606.explanation: The VVD builds with balance; no total ban outside the city. VVD wants to build in existing neighborhoods with expansions at the edges.

126608.explanation: The VVD wants more parking spaces in and around the center. Parking garages at the town edges allow for more greenery inside the city and also prevent driving around the city looking for a spot.

126554.explanation: Referendums are not a solution for complex political issues. In case of conflicting interests, the municipal council, as guardian of the general interest, must make decisions after careful consideration. We are open to new forms of citizen participation provided they have well-formulated questions and support.

126557.explanation: People also live in the city center. There is a limit to nuisance caused by noisy nightlife: the current opening hours. These provide clear, enforceable rules. The ChristenUnie values Sunday rest and wants to initiate a public debate about the importance of rest in the coming council period.

126559.explanation: Wageningen is too far from the highway for a distribution center. Additionally, the available space is limited and can be better used for examples such as nature or local amenities.

126562.explanation: Wood stoves produce fine particulate matter and thus cause damage not only to the climate but also to health. Therefore, wood stoves are prohibited in new buildings. For existing homes, wood stoves are discouraged, and users are actively informed about the Wood Burning Guide.

126563.explanation: Terrace heaters consume a lot of electricity and mainly heat the outside air. There are good alternatives to stay warm on a terrace, such as blankets and electrically heated cushions. This way, we combine comfort with responsible energy use.

126564.explanation: A light show is a beautiful alternative to a fireworks display and is less harmful to the environment. This has already been done in recent years, for example, on the night of May 4th to 5th at Hotel De Wereld.

126565.explanation: Waste is also a valuable raw material. It must remain possible for residents to bring waste free of charge to the waste disposal station Het Waardepunt. They also collect it free at home, but not unlimited, so costs and usage remain manageable.

126566.explanation: The ChristenUnie has already raised attention to this in the past period, for two reasons: to prevent hearing damage for visitors and to limit nuisance for the surroundings.

126567.explanation: The municipality of Wageningen must help entrepreneurs to offer people with a distance to the labor market and refugees a place within their company. This not only creates employment but also gives people hope and perspective. Therefore, we encourage and support social entrepreneurs.

126568.explanation: The municipality is obligated to finance a local broadcaster. For this, we receive money from the national government. No additional funds are required.

126569.explanation: The municipality must invest in school buildings that require it. The Margrietschool and De Zwaneridder (Free School) have received new buildings in recent years and do not currently need improvements. In the coming period, 2-3 other schools will be next.

126571.explanation: Sports clubs must manage responsible financial policies independently. The municipality supports indirectly, for example, by reimbursing a Certificate of Conduct and maintaining facilities. We want to encourage residents to participate in sports and remove financial barriers through the Social Participation Budget.

126573.explanation: More funding is needed for public order officers so they can address issues like drugs, illegal parking, dog fouling, litter and pollution in public spaces, street intimidation or nuisance, and bicycle nuisance in the shopping street.

126574.explanation: Currently, there are no 'hotspots' within the municipality of Wageningen where camera surveillance would add enough value to justify such an investment. On special days, such as May 5th, we agree with temporary camera surveillance due to increased security risks.

126576.explanation: There is not much aggression towards enforcement officers in Wageningen to justify equipping them with batons. To avoid escalating situations, we do not want to give public order officers batons.

126577.explanation: A starter loan sometimes gives just the push needed to buy your first home. There must be homes affordable for starters (including with a starter loan), but this requires considerable building for starters and seniors (who by moving can create housing flow).

126578.explanation: ChristenUnie wants at least 50% of the housing stock to be allocated to people who have ties with the city. This is the nationally set maximum.

126582.explanation: There is only a limited shortage of student housing in Wageningen. ChristenUnie believes municipal funds must be used efficiently and housing fairly distributed so that students and other groups, such as young people after their studies, find suitable housing.

126583.explanation: We trust that every resident participates in society according to their ability. After a three-year experiment with welfare in Wageningen, we have seen that trust works. Checking a box in the system cannot be the goal of participation policy.

126585.explanation: ChristenUnie stands for a generous poverty policy. The current income security policy is still relatively new. Therefore, we believe it is important to critically and constructively monitor the approach to this policy. Where necessary, we will actively adjust so that no one falls through the cracks.

126588.explanation: To increase livability and traffic safety, all residential streets must have a 30 km/h speed limit. For traffic flow, 50 km/h remains the speed on the ‘Ruit’ (Ritzema Bosweg, Diedenweg, Nijenoord Allee, Kortenoord Allee) and on Hollandseweg in Wageningen-Hoog.

126589.explanation: Parking permits will be introduced where there are parking problems, not preventively.

126590.explanation: Research shows this reduces CO2 emissions in the center by 43%. This is beneficial for people in the inner city and for projects that can more easily obtain permits because of this. Good coordination with entrepreneurs and residents of this zone, and supportive transition measures, are required.

126591.explanation: It is important that everyone feels comfortable with the caregivers who help them. Freedom of choice contributes to dignity, trust, and a better care experience. However, it is inevitable that choosing a specific caregiver sometimes leads to longer waiting times.

126593.explanation: For people on a limited income, there is the Social Participation Budget. Female hygiene products can also be purchased from this budget. Besides this, ChristenUnie wants to encourage initiatives that prevent period poverty, such as giveaway cabinets.

126594.explanation: From 2028, municipalities will receive significantly less money from the national government. ChristenUnie considers an increase in property tax negotiable when it appears that it is not possible to maintain facilities, but wants to keep the burden on residents and businesses as low as possible.

126595.explanation: The municipality incurs enforcement costs related to nuisance caused by dog fouling and invests in dog walking areas. It is reasonable that these costs are recovered from dog owners via the dog tax, provided a reasonable amount is applied.

126596.explanation: Starting in 2028, municipalities will receive significantly less funding from the national government. It might then be useful to raise the tourist tax to maintain facilities, but this is not automatic.

126597.explanation: The current priority system strikes a good balance between all groups in need of housing. ChristenUnie wants 50% of the housing stock to go to people with ties to Wageningen.

126599.explanation: Rooftops must initially be used for solar panels. For solar parks, we maintain the guidelines established in 2019 in the Countryside Vision to strengthen biodiversity in Binnenveld and to ensure participation of local residents and other Wageningen citizens.

126601.explanation: ChristenUnie does not accept drugs in society. People are too valuable to lose themselves in addictions to alcohol, smoking, drugs, sex, gaming, and/or gambling. Addiction is not freedom.

126603.explanation: ChristenUnie sees possibilities for high-rise buildings, adding extra floors, or plot and home splitting, provided this does not increase parking pressure and that livability, greenery, and social cohesion in the neighborhood are preserved.

126604.explanation: ChristenUnie wants to prevent multiple houses in one block from being converted into student housing. This does not benefit neighborhood livability. Therefore, we stick to current policy. Collective living arrangements are important. They promote mutual contact, companionship, and neighborly help.

126606.explanation: We see no room for large-scale expansion in Binnenveld or De Eng. That nature is too important for relaxation. Only in the 'armpit' between Haarweg, the solar park, and Nude do we see limited possibilities, mainly for social owner-occupied and rental housing with low-rise buildings and lots of greenery.

126608.explanation: In the center, we see room for greening and bicycle parking by building a parking garage on the edge of the center. A clearly marked walking route from the parking garage to Hoogstraat should also be part of this.

126554.explanation: CDA Wageningen is not in favor of referenda because only the majority's voice is reflected, which is insufficient for making a balanced decision with careful consideration of interests. However, we see a resident council as a good form of resident participation.

126557.explanation: That is already possible now and we consider it a good thing, with the opinions of staff and customers already taken into account properly at this time.

126559.explanation: There is little space in Wageningen, which makes it unattractive to place large storage centers for (web) shops here.

126562.explanation: We oppose a ban. However, we believe we should focus well on awareness about the times when burning wood is appropriate. Wood burning is not possible under all weather conditions. The damage caused by wood burning to lung patients and neighbors depends strongly on the weather.

126563.explanation: We believe it is up to entrepreneurs themselves to consider whether they find it responsible to use terrace heating. Customers can also decide for themselves whether or not to sit there if they consider it an important point.

126564.explanation: A fireworks show can become an alternative to setting off fireworks. In our municipality, other parties only support a light show; we also think it is acceptable if there is some fireworks included.

126565.explanation: In the current system, you can have bulky waste collected for free using points on your waste pass. This is sufficient for three disposals per year. As a result, residents are less dependent on having their own car or need to make fewer trips with the available cargo bike at ACV.

126566.explanation: Festivals and parties belong to the city. A certain degree of noise nuisance sometimes comes with that. At the moment, we find the standards appropriate. Organizers must involve nearby residents in large events so they can contribute ideas on noise reduction measures in time.

126567.explanation: Entrepreneurs have their own responsibility to comply with laws and regulations. Accessibility for people with disabilities is part of that. The municipality does have a role in appropriately designing public spaces and thereby access to businesses and shops.

126568.explanation: The local broadcaster is a great medium supported by volunteers. The municipality currently contributes to this and does not need to do more. We find it important that it is supported by residents and aided by the municipality.

126569.explanation: The municipality of Wageningen is responsible for the quality of school buildings. It fulfills this responsibility. For example, the primary schools De Bijenkorf, De Zwaneridder, and the Margrietschool have been newly built. More schools are scheduled for new construction or improvement in the coming years.

126571.explanation: We support assistance for people who are financially unable to participate in sports, but we do not see supporting the clubs themselves as a government task. Members have a responsibility towards the club and in a healthy club can solve problems together.

126573.explanation: The deployment of enforcement officers (BOAs) is important. But there is no need to expand this unless research shows that the current deployment is insufficient.

126574.explanation: At this moment, cameras may only be used in public spaces on May 4 and 5. We favor extending this to other large-scale events to increase visitor safety.

126576.explanation: A BOA is not a police officer; we believe it is important that the monopoly on violence remains with the police. A police officer is more trained than a BOA and can better assess what a situation requires.

126577.explanation: The municipality is committed to sufficient affordable housing. There are enough national initiatives or special starters’ mortgages at banks for financing. We see that starter loans in many municipalities mostly lead to higher house prices.

126578.explanation: Everyone who wants to live in Wageningen is welcome. But due to the housing shortage, we want to ensure that people who are socially or economically tied to Wageningen get priority. By law, a maximum of 50% of freed-up homes can be allocated locally. The CDA wants to utilize that space.

126582.explanation: In the city of Wageningen, we have a professional student housing provider, Idealis. We believe organizing student housing is their task.

126583.explanation: Poverty is not combated by money alone. It is important that everyone sees that they can mean something for another. By encouraging people who are long-term unemployed to do something for others, they gain self-confidence which may also help them find paid work in the long term.

126585.explanation: We consider the current threshold appropriate. The threshold is based on calculations by experts in poverty at the Nibud.

126588.explanation: When you reduce the speed limit everywhere to 30 km/h, it causes more detour traffic, which affects the safety of cyclists and children. For traffic flow and safety, it is important that the speed limit remains 50 km/h on main roads around the city.

126589.explanation: Introducing a parking permit leads to displacement of parking pressure to the immediate surroundings. Further expansion is not a solution to parking pressure.

126590.explanation: This measure would especially accelerate costs for small businesses that have not yet replaced their company vans. An environmental zone is not necessary; polluting vehicles will disappear on their own over time.

126591.explanation: Dutch law requires care offices and health insurers to take account of diversity in offerings during contracting. We also find this important. But this has nothing to do with municipal policy.

126593.explanation: We appreciate that associations, churches, and citizens provide this support. However, we do not see this as a task for the municipality.

126594.explanation: The municipality of Wageningen has a relatively high property tax (OZB) rate compared to the national average. So there is no need to increase it for now; we believe that in case of deficits, we should first look at our expenditures.

126595.explanation: Dogs are pleasant companions but also a burden on public spaces. The municipality invests in dog-walking facilities, poop bags, and maintenance of public greenery. It is only fair that dog owners contribute to this.

126596.explanation: Wageningen has relatively little tourism, which generates little revenue. For small deficits, a targeted increase in tourist tax can be considered because we suspect it has little impact on tourist numbers.

126597.explanation: Anyone who is unsafe at home or has no (temporary) home deserves priority for social housing. Status holders are recognized refugees and fall into this category. That is why they get priority along with other urgent groups. We want no one in these groups to end up on the street.

126599.explanation: The CDA prefers solar panels on roofs, not on agricultural land.

126601.explanation: The CDA does not want to normalize drug use and opposes expanding the number of coffee shops.

126603.explanation: To meet the housing demand in the Netherlands, we cannot do without stacked (multi-story) housing. This can and fits in Wageningen too. However, it is important to make good agreements about integrating high-rise buildings.

126604.explanation: There is a housing shortage and dividing houses can actually lead to more homes. Moreover, mixed living arrangements are also good for social cohesion.

126606.explanation: Because the housing shortage is very large, space is needed for housing. There is limited space in the rural area. Many areas are protected nature where building is not allowed. But there are places where building is permitted, for example in the wedge between Haarweg and the Gamma.

126608.explanation: The current number of parking spaces is sufficient. No more are needed. Nor should there be fewer! As far as we are concerned, the agreement is that if parking spaces in the center are removed, they must be added elsewhere in the center.

126554.explanation: In our view, the citizen's council is very important in participation policy so that residents can engage in dialogue together resulting in well-considered advice and decisions. We also believe that advisory referendums belong to important decisions, such as large construction projects or municipal reorganizations.

126557.explanation: We believe that opening hours should be part of the permit process. We always want to consider the neighbors and limit noise nuisance.

126559.explanation: New distribution centers do not fit in our municipality. We want to oppose so-called 'industrialization' and therefore reject large-scale distribution centers and data centers.

126562.explanation: Wood smoke causes a lot of fine particulate matter and health problems for many residents. Therefore, we want a ban on wood burning. Moreover, we want the municipality to establish a reporting point where complaints about smoke nuisance can be made. This will also inform residents about the disadvantages of wood burning.

126563.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to eliminate energy-consuming devices such as patio heaters. We strive for a policy where the municipality works with local entrepreneurs to minimize energy waste (such as patio heaters and open shop doors).

126564.explanation: Organized shows offer opportunities for social connection. We welcome festive, safe, and environmentally friendly alternatives to fireworks, such as shows with light, music, drones, and lasers—of course only without nuisance to people, animals, nature, and the environment. We support a fireworks-free Wageningen!

126565.explanation: The polluter pays. This means residents who produce less waste pay lower costs. Collection or drop-off of bulky waste remains free up to nine times per year.

126566.explanation: Both animals and residents can experience a lot of nuisance from festivals and large events. Therefore, we believe the municipality should establish stricter rules to combat noise nuisance and light pollution. Parties should be possible but with consideration for people, animals, and the surrounding nature.

126567.explanation: We strongly focus on accessibility for everyone. Therefore, we want the municipality to take a leading role in making existing buildings accessible.

126568.explanation: We believe strong, independent local and regional media are of great importance for the residents of Wageningen. Therefore, we will not cut funding here. To promote independent (investigative) journalism, we want a media fund for non-commercial local media.

126569.explanation: A healthy indoor climate in schools is important for both students and teachers. Therefore, we believe the municipality must allocate sufficient funds to achieve this. Think of nature-inclusive renovation and sustainability improvements of outdated school buildings.

126571.explanation: Sports clubs are a good place for connection and social cohesion. If these clubs need support, we will provide it.

126573.explanation: We believe the municipality should invest more in street workers, neighborhood parents, and community police officers: the eyes and ears of the neighborhood. Thanks to their efforts, potential problems can be noticed early on before they escalate. So, less punishment and more preventive care and contact.

126574.explanation: Camera surveillance provides a false sense of security and merely shifts the problem elsewhere. Therefore, we want to be very cautious about using cameras in public spaces. Automatic facial recognition is too invasive and, as far as we are concerned, should not be used.

126576.explanation: We support more prevention and care with a human approach. Heavier arming of enforcement officers does not fit with this.

126577.explanation: The Party for the Animals wants to help first-time buyers find affordable housing but uses other means such as more social rentals, affordable owner-occupied homes, and active land policy. A loan does not solve the problem but merely shifts or enlarges it.

126578.explanation: Everyone deserves affordable housing. Attention must be paid to the most vulnerable groups, such as people without housing. Making distinctions based on where residents previously lived only worsens the housing crisis for others.

126582.explanation: Flexible housing can provide relief in the current housing crisis but does not solve it. We find it important that students are decently housed and the municipality can assist with that. For flexible housing, the municipality must support initiatives for circular construction.

126583.explanation: Residents receiving social assistance are in that position for a reason. No performance requirements should be attached. If residents on social assistance wish to contribute something extra to the city, they can do so through unpaid work, such as volunteering.

126585.explanation: We want the municipality to continue evaluating and improving the Social Participation Budget so that this scheme is more generous than the arrangements it replaces. We adhere to 130% of the social minimum and will increase it if needed.

126588.explanation: We strive for a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h within built-up areas. This is safer and results in cleaner air. The road layout will be adjusted where possible to also create extra space for greenery.

126589.explanation: We want to expand the paid parking permit zones in the municipality. For the second and subsequent parking permits, you pay more. This is a step toward a car-reduced Wageningen and creates more space for nature in neighborhoods and the city center.

126590.explanation: We support an environmental zone in the city center. This ensures cleaner air and thus better health for our residents.

126591.explanation: Appropriate care for everyone is important. Care providers and shelter locations must always take into account users' background, identity, lifestyle, and related needs. This way, care becomes accessible and safe for everyone.

126593.explanation: Period poverty causes stress, health complaints, and unequal opportunities. By distributing menstrual products through social services such as community centers and food banks, we combat period poverty. Everyone deserves a safe and hygienic menstruation.

126594.explanation: We want to increase property tax (OZB) only for more expensive homes; the heaviest burdens should be borne by the wealthiest. We want to link property tax to the energy label of homes, so inefficient homes pay higher tax. Subsidies can help make homes more sustainable.

126595.explanation: It is unfair and outdated to treat a living animal as a taxable object. Moreover, this creates a distinction between dog owners and owners of other pets. As long as the dog tax exists, its revenue should go exclusively to facilities for dogs.

126596.explanation: Tourism causes significant damage to the climate and nature due to associated polluting transport and overconsumption. Therefore, the municipality uses tourist tax revenues to promote sustainability and strengthen nature to limit tourism's damage.

126597.explanation: The municipality must help refugees find housing, suitable jobs, and/or education. Wageningen opposes the bill that would remove priority for status holders in social housing. If the law passes, the municipality will establish a local priority scheme.

126599.explanation: There will be no solar parks in nature areas. Agricultural land is not intended for solar meadows but for plant-based food production and nature development, and if livestock farming shrinks considerably, for housing construction.

126601.explanation: Assigning new coffee shop locations must be done carefully and under clear conditions. The Party for the Animals is not opposed to coffee shops but they must not open near schools.

126603.explanation: We want more high residential towers in Wageningen, while keeping an eye on the livability of humans and animals. Think of sufficient space between buildings, greenery, and good facilities. The high-rise buildings should fit into the environment and have at least one green facade.

126604.explanation: Tenants must be better protected. We are not against renting out single rooms, but stricter rules for good landlordship are needed, with license revocation upon violation. Special attention will be given to discrimination and intimidation.

126606.explanation: To keep Wageningen livable, we must cherish existing greenery. We cannot solve the housing crisis by worsening the biodiversity crisis since that leaves us equally far from home. There are plenty of alternatives.

126608.explanation: No more new car parking spaces should be added in the city center. We believe the municipality should focus more on creating space for greenery and/or bicycle parking. For a green and healthy city.

statements.title: The municipality should ban new construction in green areas.
In Wageningen the use of fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed.

titleParts.text: In Wageningen moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: The property tax (ozb) should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.

statements.theme: Closing times for bars and restaurants
statements.title: Cafés and restaurants must be able to determine their own opening hours.
The municipality should distribute tampons and sanitary pads for free.
There should be more camera surveillance in Wageningen.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Wageningen should introduce parking permits in more neighborhoods.

titleParts.text: Wageningen moet in meer wijken een

titleParts.glossary: A permit to park in a certain place. You have to pay for that right.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Residents of Wageningen should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Wageningen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Wageningen should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who deal with safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in temporary housing for students, for example container housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in tijdelijke huisvesting voor studenten, bijvoorbeeld

titleParts.glossary: Moveable and temporary homes, usually made from a shipping container.

statements.title: The BOAs who enforce safety in Wageningen should be issued a baton.

titleParts.glossary: Special Enforcement Officers. They can issue fines and often supervise the streets.

titleParts.text: die in Wageningen controleren op veiligheid moeten een wapenstok krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Wageningen should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

titleParts.glossary: An area where very polluting cars are not allowed to drive.

titleParts.text: invoeren om vervuilende voertuigen uit het centrum te weren.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should block the opening of a new coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
More parking spaces should be created in and around the center of Wageningen.

shootoutStatements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
The municipality must ban outdoor patio heaters.
The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.
The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.
More tall residential apartment blocks should be allowed in Wageningen.
